item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this a few years ago before I knew anything about the site (like, just randomly found it) and it really left an impression on me, I guess it resonated with me in a way I hadn't really experienced before. It's something I still think about from time to time (if you couldn't tell haha) so… thank you for making gay trashman :_)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I don't see what some people have against it. :)
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gay trashman is also trashman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reads title

Guys I'm not that anomalous, c'mon.

item.title: oof
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was a joke at first so i clicked it,
But fucking hell, that's a sad story, fucking dude just wanted to be with the one he loved.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea that he's too regretful to go back, but it feels a bit hollow when we can't guess or speculate as to why. There doesn't have to be a big explanation; more notes with some hints would work, to detail his relationship to his husband/boyfriend/???.

What I'm saying is, consider making him gayer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one doesn't do much for me. I really want to like him because all of the ideas in the article are intriguing but none of them are given enough attention.

As a character story there just isn't enough character here for me to really get invested in his troubles at all. They're just explained to me. The letters help a bit, but there's not much to those either.

And as an SCP his anomalous properties are woefully underexplored. It gives me the impression that he's only anomalous at all because of he wasn't anomalous he couldn't be an SCP. He needs some reason to be an article and some reason the foundation can't take him off the island (although even THAT doesn't really matter all that much) and otherwise it doesn't matter what he can do or why.

All of the pieces of this SCP just feel like they're fluff surrounding a bigger story or reveal but that bigger story never comes, unfortunately.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just a love story, this isn't anything compelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The explanation for the title is in the subthread above your post.

Also, you should edit your comments instead of posting multiple in a row like this. You can go to Options —> Edit to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The scp itself was fine, btw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, but why is its title "the gay trash man". Like, is that his defining characteristic? Does being gay somehow give him ghost powers? I don't care one way or the other about if a character is gay, straight, or anything. It is sad that you couldn't come up with a better description of your scp than "gay trash man". Let me tell you a secret: him being gay has practically no significance. If you actually think that the title should stay as it is, why don't we just label EVERY HUMANOID SCP BASED ON THEIR ORIENTATION!?! THAT WOULD BE GREAT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except he is pining for a Mark. That's what a large part of his story is about. So the title works. Plus being able to change the gender of the romantic interest doesn't mean his sexuality isn't valid. Him being a trashman has nothing to do with his anomaly either, yet no one has a problem with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree about the title. His sexuality has nothing to do with his anomalousness. The Ethereal Trashman could be pining for Marcie instead of Mark and it would be exactly the same story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no reason for him not to be gay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
haven't checked the comment section of this page for a while, so i missed this one.

here's your answer: he's gay because why the fuck shouldn't he be?

edit: also, i find it hilarious that you’re accusing a lesbian of jumping on the lgbt bandwagon. i’ve seen a lot of silly shit in my day but i think that one takes the cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: he's gay because some people are gay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really sure of this skip. I like the idea of it, but what does being gay have to do with him. Like, why cant he just be the "ethereal trashman" I feel like him being gay is just trying to jump on the "lgbt bandwagon"

in my opinion it doesnt add anything to his character. If the author is still around, or if i have missed something relevant, please correct me-not trying to start a flame war.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's one way to avoid your problems, I guess! I thought this was marvelous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's not anomalous, it's just his character.

All in all, I really did like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
has been translated into Chinese

http://scp-wiki-cn.wikidot.com/scp-3915
And I loved this skip too.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for sure

I loved this. I honestly don't know about adding detail. The article feels really different and part of that, for me, is the sparseness. I was reminded (for obvious reasons) of when I used to live by a beach and spent time there right before sunrise - there were always bits of trash left behind, sometimes pecked at by a seagull or two, painting a tiny but mostly empty picture of whoever had been there the previous night. It seems appropriate for the evidence in this article to feel like only the few hollow scraps that were left behind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this skip would benefit from a few clandestine surveillance logs that show some of -3915's character outside of the interview.

That aside, I also like the idea of there being a PoI/GoI out there handing out magic artifacts/powers to people with the intent of them doing good with them. It could be perverted by this PoI/GoI making bad decisions with who they give things out to, but also opens the door for anomalous, creepy, but in the end well-meaning and even positive, narratives.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great idea but I wish you could have gone into more detail when talking about what happened between him and his partner.

with that said +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Trashman is still gay.
Gummy_Dragon still likes the skip.
+1

item.title: Critique
item.content:encoded:
+ Show nitpicks
- Hide nitpicks

SCP-3915 was virtually identical to an ordinary human male except for its incorporeal nature

If it's still only 'virtually' identical without its anomalous properties, surely that'd mean it had something else abnormal about it. Why not just say 'SCP-3915 was identical […] except for its incorporeal nature'?

"a gift from a friend".

I do not like this. This, combined with the "[DESTROYED]" later on, just feels like a lazy excuse to avoid having to convey certain parts of the story. That may just be me, but it's quite a large downside to an otherwise adequate article. Sorry :/

a written document that read 'gone to do what should have been done years ago'

Spooky ghost leaves cryptic clues that are still blindingly obvious, making me wonder what the point of making it an un-capitalised sentence fragment even was.

okay. i know you're not coast guard. it's fine.

Again with this. Why does this person go into the trouble of dotting capital Is incorrectly, but still uses apostrophes in the correct places. It seems like you're making it more 'realistic' by making this person seem stupid (in a weirdly specific way).

Overall I agree with Kalinin above: there's nothing that makes me feel for the character, and the way the letters were written distracted me from their contents. The idea doesn't seem distinct from any other incorporeal humanoids we have on the site, and I never found myself 'involved' with the narrative. The core ideas are good, but I think you could come up with a much deeper backstory than "we fought" and a much better anomaly than 'ghost (actually not a ghost)'.

Reluctant downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the second article I’ve read today where as a reader I’m expected to feel something for a character that is only barely presented through the story.

“This exists and it’s sad/heartwarming/tragic/etc. because it exists” has never been effective for me. The most insight we get into the protagonist is through a brief, surly interview, which makes me completely numb to his romantic plight, which I’m led to believe is the whole reason behind this.

I feel like if you’re going to expect the reader to identify and feel something, you’ve got to do more to forge that connection than what’s presented here. Notes at the end aren’t cutting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i agree

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gay trashman is gay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't get invested in this, primarily because I felt the whole things was rather shallow, We're only offered the vaguest glimpse of this person and what happened between them and their partner, and his motivations don't come off as poignant as they could. It feels far too direct, to the point of being almost cloying in the first and third collapsibles.

I think diving deeper into this, giving us more of their story, show how he grows and adapts over time, show his attitude and feelings (instead of blatantly "telling" throughout the logs) could give this more weight than it currently has.

As it stands, I can't feel anything for the character. I could see myself upvoting a strong character piece in this vein, but this is't it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Relevant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upbooted this no matter what "Gay trash monster" is just… it needs to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This touched me. I liked it, a lot. +1

item.title: Authorpost
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who critted this, including SunnyClockwork, Darkstuff, and all the rest :D

Image edited from here, which is creative commons and also the only suitable picture I could find of this isolated damn island.

Happy reading!
